meta.question: What is the correct layout, z-index structure, and UX flow for the grainulator.app demo page — covering desktop and mobile, landing-to-demo transition, model loading states, and claim card rendering — given that the lava lamp shader must remain untouched?
meta.audience: Aid — the engineer who built the ecosystem, testing on desktop Chrome and mobile Safari

claims.text: The lava lamp WebGL shader animation must remain untouched — it is the brand identity of the page.
Output is a single self-contained HTML file (site/index.html) deployed to GitHub Pages. No build step, no external CSS/JS dependencies beyond WebLLM CDN.

Must support two tracks: Track A (pre-generated demo, instant, no model needed) and Track B (live local inference via WebLLM). Track A is the fallback for mobile, no-WebGPU, slow networks, and while model loads.
Everything except the lava lamp is open to change — layout, z-index, element positions, transitions, button placement, footer, glass morphism approach.
Current demo overlay does not properly hide landing UI elements — mascot, install button, compile button, and footer all bleed through, creating a broken layered mess on both desktop and mobile.
Track A claim cards are not rendering in the demo view — the claims area appears empty despite loadTrackA() being called. Massive empty space between question input and bottom elements.
Model loading has no progress indicator visible to the user — 'model still loading' text gives no timeline, percentage, or size estimate. User has no idea if it will take 5 seconds or 5 minutes.
Transparent/glass background on demo overlay is distracting — lava lamp blobs showing through the demo content area compete for attention with claim cards and text.
.ui.hidden uses opacity:0 + pointer-events:none (line 106), NOT display:none. Elements still render in the paint layer and show through any translucent overlay above them.

demo-layer::before background uses radial gradients at 0.10-0.15 opacity with transparent falloff — only the linear-gradient at bottom reaches 0.9. The center of the viewport is barely darkened, making landing elements visible behind it.

loadTrackA() fires immediately in enterDemo() (line 804) while demo-layer is still mid-transition (0.5s opacity). The demo-layer transitions from overflow:hidden to overflow-y:auto at the same time — cards may be clipped or have animation race conditions during this window.

The bottom section stacks mascot + title + cta-row + cancel button + privacy note all in a single position:fixed container at bottom:6vh. Cancel button is outside the flex row. On mobile, cta-row switches to flex-direction:column. This creates visual clutter.

FIX: Use display:none on .ui.hidden and .site-footer.hidden instead of opacity:0. This completely removes elements from rendering during demo state, eliminating bleed-through regardless of overlay opacity.

FIX: Make demo-layer::before nearly opaque — use rgba(0,0,0,0.95) solid background instead of translucent gradient soup. The lava lamp already shows through the dimmed canvas (opacity:0.3); adding another layer of translucency on top is double-translucency that helps nothing.

FIX: Move loadTrackA() call inside the transitionend handler (line 797) so cards render AFTER the demo layer is fully visible and overflow-y:auto is established. This eliminates the animation race condition.
FIX: Use 100dvh instead of 100vh for demo-ui min-height to handle mobile dynamic viewport height (address bar collapse). Add safe-area-inset-bottom to demo-ui bottom padding.

CONFIRMED: Bug 1 — Video placeholder (lines 395-398) has no JS references. No getElementById, querySelector, or event listener targets it. Safe to remove. The cancel button (id=cancel-btn, line 407) is currently a sibling after .cta-row; moving it inside .cta-row is safe because .cta-row uses display:flex with flex-wrap:wrap and center alignment (line 110), so the cancel button will inherit the flex layout correctly. The button already has display:none by default and only shows during model download.

CHALLENGED: Bug 2 — Raising fuzzy threshold from 0.15 to 0.35 is too aggressive. The scoring formula is 0.7*wordOverlap + 0.3*(1-levDistance). For 'database migration' against 'PostgreSQL vs CockroachDB for a growing SaaS with multi-region ambitions', word overlap is ~0 (no shared significant words) so score would be near 0.0-0.1. But 'monorepo vs polyrepo' against a monorepo demo entry would share 'monorepo' and 'polyrepo' — with 2/3 word overlap that gives ~0.47, safely above 0.35. A threshold of 0.25 is better: it still blocks 'how to cook pasta' (score ~0.0) while keeping partial-match queries like 'database scaling options' (which might share 1-2 words with demo entries scoring ~0.2-0.3). The fallback behavior (show toast + keep existing claims) is correct and should ship.

CHALLENGED: Bug 3 — The prescription says 'load Track A FIRST, THEN start attract mode AFTER 3s delay.' But enterDemo() already does this (line 853: loadTrackA(), line 855: startAttractMode()). The real bug is that runAttractDemo() immediately clears area.innerHTML (line 2185) on its first run, wiping the Track A claims. The fix is: (1) add a 3s setTimeout before the first runAttractDemo(0) call inside startAttractMode(), AND (2) attract mode MUST stop if the user types or clicks (check: stopAttractMode is called on input keydown at line 2265, confirmed). However, the crossfade idea is overkill — just delaying 3s before the first attract wipe is sufficient. The user interaction guard already exists.

CHALLENGED: Bug 4 — CRITICAL: The canvas uses `c.getContext('webgl')` with NO options object (line 463). WebGL's default for preserveDrawingBuffer is FALSE. When the rAF loop stops, the browser is free to clear the drawing buffer at any time (typically on the next composite). The canvas WILL go blank or show garbage on most browsers. Two options: (1) Add `{ preserveDrawingBuffer: true }` to getContext — costs ~5-10% GPU perf due to no buffer swap optimization, or (2) keep canvas.style.display='none' but set a CSS background-color on the canvas to match the last dominant color. Option 1 is cleaner. The prescription as written WILL cause a blank canvas.

CONFIRMED with caveat: Bug 5 — 'New sprint' button in demo header is the right UX. resetExploreState() (line 1394) properly clears _allClaims, _turnCount, _nextClaimIndex, _conversationHistory, _compilationResult, _isFirstTurn, _liveClaims, and removes turnCounter. BUT it does NOT clear the claims area DOM, the compiler output, or re-enable/focus the input. The 'Ask another question' button (line 1713-1727) does all of that. The new button handler must replicate the full cleanup from lines 1714-1726: resetExploreState() + clearSession() + clear qInput + focus + clear compiler output + clear claims area. Label 'New sprint' is correct — it matches the product vocabulary.

CONFIRMED: Bug 6 — All loadDemo() call sites traced: (1) loadTrackA initial load (line 1390) — accumulate:true, should set question (first turn). (2) loadTrackA from saved session resume (line 1378) — accumulate:true, should set question. (3) Live inference Track B (line 1899) — accumulate:true, should NOT set question (user typed it). (4) Fuzzy match (line 1928) — accumulate:true, should NOT set question (user typed it). (5) Template fallback (line 1938) — accumulate:true, should NOT set question (user typed it). (6-7) Attract mode (lines 2197, 2219) — no accumulate flag, SHOULD set question (attract mode controls the display). The _isFirstTurn check is broken because it becomes false after loadTrackA, so subsequent accumulate calls still overwrite. An explicit `setQuestion` option replacing the _isFirstTurn logic is the correct fix. Attract calls: setQuestion:true. User-initiated calls (3,4,5): setQuestion:false. Initial loads (1,2): setQuestion:true.

CONFIRMED: Bug 7 — All 74 `var` declarations in the file are inside function bodies (scoreEntry, significantWords, fuzzyMatchDemo, templateFallback, loadDemo, updateTurnCounter, resetExploreState, generateFollowUpChips, etc.) or inside isSoftwareRenderer. None rely on cross-block hoisting — they are all simple local variables within their function scope. Converting var->let/const via biome is safe. The only vars in the global-like scope are inside isSoftwareRenderer (lines 470, 472) which is a function. biome's --write flag will also fix formatting and remove unused vars, which is safe for a single HTML file with all JS inline. No runtime behavior change expected.

CONFLICT: preserveDrawingBuffer (Bug 4). Live code (line 461) has preserveDrawingBuffer:true (shipped in 898264b). Pass 7 witness w2506 says revert this and use toDataURL snapshot instead. However, the toDataURL pattern is NOT implemented. pauseShader() calls cancelAnimationFrame which stops rendering — without preserveDrawingBuffer, the canvas goes blank on pause (enterDemo calls pauseShader at line 845). Reverting preserveDrawingBuffer WITHOUT shipping the snapshot overlay will BREAK the pause-motion feature. These two changes are coupled: revert + snapshot must ship atomically.

RESOLUTION: preserveDrawingBuffer. Ship as atomic pair: (1) Remove preserveDrawingBuffer:true from getContext, (2) In pauseShader(), before cancelAnimationFrame, render one frame, call canvas.toDataURL(), create an img overlay positioned over the canvas, show it. In resumeShader(), remove the overlay img. This gives zero ongoing GPU cost while preserving visual continuity on pause. Until this pair ships, keep preserveDrawingBuffer:true — it works, just wastes GPU.

CONSISTENT: Fuzzy threshold evolution. Live code: significantWords filters w.length > 3 (line 1015), threshold is 0.25 (line 1087), NO zero-overlap gate exists (scoreEntry returns 0.3*(1-levDist) when shared=0). Pass 4 identified root cause as tie-breaking at index 0 when zero words overlap. Pass 5 resolved: ship 2-line fix (word filter >1, overlap gate if shared===0 return 0), keep threshold at 0.25. No contradictions — the passes built on each other correctly. w2421 (tags) was superseded by x531 (algorithmic fix).

CRITICAL COUPLING: Rx1 + Rx6 must ship together. Live code line 1947 has _allClaims.length > 0 guard that returns early with toast instead of falling through to templateFallback(). The x531 overlap gate (if shared===0 return 0) makes fuzzyMatchDemo return null MORE often. Without removing the guard at line 1947, those null returns hit the early-return toast. Shipping Rx1 alone makes UX WORSE — queries that used to get a poor fuzzy match now get nothing. Cannot ship Rx1 as a standalone test.

CONFIRMED: Template fallback guard incompatible with solo Rx1 test. The guard at line 1947 (if _allClaims.length > 0, show toast and return) means: after Track A loads, _allClaims has 5 claims, guard fires, templateFallback() is unreachable. Pass 3 witness w2438 says remove it. Pass 5 x540 says Rx1+Rx6 must ship together. Both agree. The fix is: remove the early return at line 1947-1949, always fall through to templateFallback() when fuzzy returns null.

ATTRACT MODE: Three bugs identified across passes, all independent fixes. (1) Orphaned setTimeout at line 2174 — uses raw setTimeout, NOT attractTimeout(). If user interrupts during 3s delay, this timeout fires after stopAttractMode clears attractTimeouts. Fix: change to attractTimeout(). (2) Compile button not in onUserInteraction — listeners at lines 2289-2298 cover question input, claims area, and keydown, but NOT compileBtn click. User clicking compile during attract gets wiped. Fix: add compileBtn click listener. (3) 3s delay too short. Fix: increase to 5000ms. All three are independent 1-line fixes.

ATTRACT MODE FSM EXIT: stopAttractMode() (lines 2144-2154) clears timeouts and removes indicator/replay button, but does NOT re-render _allClaims to DOM. After attract mode overwrites claimsArea innerHTML (line 2193), stopping attract leaves the DOM showing the last attract demo's claims while _allClaims still holds Track A claims. Next user action that reads _allClaims sees Track A but DOM shows attract content = state desync. Fix: add to stopAttractMode() after line 2153: re-render _allClaims to claimsArea using the same forEach pattern from session restore (lines 1342-1355). This is independent of the 3 bug fixes in x006.

ORDERING: Attract mode fixes have no ordering constraints. x006 (3 bug fixes) and x007 (FSM exit action) modify different lines in the attract mode section. x006 touches line 2174, interrupt listeners at 2289-2298, and the 3000ms constant. x007 adds lines inside stopAttractMode() at 2144-2154. No conflicts. Can ship in any order or together in one commit.

HANDOFF: Live code has dual handoff (deep link + clipboard). Commit 898264b replaced old buttons. Lines 1640-1676 build the post-compile handoff with two buttons: 'Open in Claude Code' (deep link via buildHandoffDeepLink at line 1231) and 'Copy to clipboard'. Lines 1253-1291 build the progressive nudge handoff (appears after N turns) with same two buttons. Pass 3 identified Web Share API and claude.ai/code as ADDITIONAL paths, not replacements. These are Batch 4 (needs design) per Pass 5 shipping order — not conflicts with current code.

HANDOFF LAYOUT RISK: Pass 5 x540 identified that Web Share + Claude.ai buttons (Rx7 + Rx9) would add 4 buttons total to handoff section, overflowing on 320px mobile. Needs platform-conditional rendering: show Web Share on mobile (navigator.share check), show deep link on desktop. This is a design decision, not a code conflict. Current dual-button layout is correct for now.

VIEWPORT-FIT: Live code line 19 has viewport meta WITHOUT viewport-fit=cover. All 5+ env(safe-area-inset-*) rules in the CSS evaluate to 0px on all devices. This has been silently broken since page creation. Pass 6 P0 fix + Pass 7 challenge confirm: add viewport-fit=cover, but MUST ship with motion toggle bottom fix (calc(16px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom,0px))) in same commit to avoid motion toggle losing iOS automatic padding.

FINAL SHIPPING ORDER (all 7 passes reconciled): Batch 0 — P0 viewport/safe-area fixes (4 LOC, must be atomic). Batch 1 — Attract bug fixes x006 (3 LOC) + FSM exit x007 (5 LOC) + SW cache cleanup (1 LOC). Batch 2 — Fuzzy overlap gate + word filter >1 + remove template guard at line 1947 (Rx1+Rx6, MUST be atomic, 3 LOC). Batch 3 — Chip handler + contrast/focus a11y fixes. Batch 4 — Web Share + Claude.ai handoff + layout work. Batch 5 — preserveDrawingBuffer revert + toDataURL snapshot (MUST be atomic). No batch has ordering dependencies on other batches except Batch 2 internals and Batch 5 internals.
